---
title: "Global Illumination: Die Physik des Lichts"
author: "Laura Barrionuevo, Nermine Bouchaala, Nikolai Straßmair"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
jupyter: python3
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Abstract
Nach @knuth1984 ist TeX...
This is a single-file Quarto website hosted on GitHub Pages. It combines Markdown text, LaTeX math, and interactive 3D elements.

```{python}
#| label: fig-polar
#| fig-cap: "A line plot on a polar axis"

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

r = np.arange(0, 2, 0.01)
theta = 2 * np.pi * r
fig, ax = plt.subplots(
  subplot_kw = {'projection': 'polar'} 
)
ax.plot(theta, r)
ax.set_rticks([0.5, 1, 1.5, 2])
ax.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

## Methodology
You can include complex math like $$E = mc^2$$ and immediately follow it with an interactive 3D figure to visualize your results.

```{=html}

<script src="https://cdn.babylonjs.com/babylon.js"></script>

<div style="width: 100%; height: 400px; position: relative; margin: 20px 0;">
    <canvas id="renderCanvas" style="width: 100%; height: 100%; touch-action: none; border: 1px solid #ddd; display: block; border-radius: 8px;"></canvas>
</div>

<script>
    window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
        const canvas = document.getElementById("renderCanvas");
        if (!canvas) return;

        const engine = new BABYLON.Engine(canvas, true);

        const createScene = function () {
            const scene = new BABYLON.Scene(engine);
            scene.clearColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.95, 0.95, 0.95);

            const camera = new BABYLON.ArcRotateCamera("camera", -Math.PI/4, Math.PI/3, 5, BABYLON.Vector3.Zero(), scene);
            camera.attachControl(canvas, true);

            const light = new BABYLON.HemisphericLight("light", new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1, 0), scene);
            
            const box = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateBox("box", {size: 1.5}, scene);
            const material = new BABYLON.StandardMaterial("mat", scene);
            material.diffuseColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.1, 0.4, 0.8);
            box.material = material;

            scene.registerBeforeRender(function () {
                box.rotation.y += 0.01;
                box.rotation.x += 0.005;
            });

            return scene;
        };

        const scene = createScene();
        engine.runRenderLoop(() => scene.render());
        window.addEventListener("resize", () => engine.resize());
    });
</script>
```

## Videospiele: Früher vs. Heute
Dieser kleine Vergleich zeigt, wie stark sich Echtzeitgrafik verändert hat: links ein stark vereinfachter Retro-Look, rechts eine modernere Darstellung mit PBR-Material, Schatten und weicherem Licht.

```{=html}
<style>
    .era-compare {
        --retro-bg: #f8f5ef;
        --modern-bg: #eaf6ff;
        --frame: #1f2937;
        margin: 1.25rem 0 2rem;
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
        gap: 1rem;
    }

    .era-card {
        border: 1px solid #d1d5db;
        border-radius: 12px;
        overflow: hidden;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08);
        background: #fff;
    }

    .era-head {
        padding: 0.65rem 0.8rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        letter-spacing: 0.02em;
        border-bottom: 1px solid #d1d5db;
    }

    .era-head.retro { background: var(--retro-bg); }
    .era-head.modern { background: var(--modern-bg); }

    .era-canvas {
        width: 100%;
        height: 300px;
        display: block;
        touch-action: none;
        background: #f3f4f6;
    }

    .era-canvas-wrap {
        position: relative;
    }

    .era-overlay {
        position: absolute;
        top: 10px;
        left: 10px;
        z-index: 2;
        padding: 0.3rem 0.55rem;
        border-radius: 999px;
        border: 1px solid rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.25);
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
        color: #111827;
        font-size: 0.74rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        letter-spacing: 0.01em;
        backdrop-filter: blur(2px);
    }

    .era-note {
        padding: 0.6rem 0.8rem 0.8rem;
        margin: 0;
        font-size: 0.92rem;
        color: #374151;
    }

    @media (max-width: 860px) {
        .era-compare {
            grid-template-columns: 1fr;
        }
    }
</style>

<div class="era-compare">
    <article class="era-card">
        <header class="era-head retro">Früher: stilisiert und unrealistisch</header>
        <div class="era-canvas-wrap">
            <span class="era-overlay">Gleiche Geometrie und Kamera</span>
            <canvas id="retroCanvas" class="era-canvas"></canvas>
        </div>
        <p class="era-note">Kaum Schatten, wenig Materialdetails.</p>
    </article>

    <article class="era-card">
        <header class="era-head modern">Heute: realistischer und dichter</header>
        <div class="era-canvas-wrap">
            <span class="era-overlay">Gleiche Geometrie und Kamera</span>
            <canvas id="modernCanvas" class="era-canvas"></canvas>
        </div>
        <p class="era-note">PBR-Materialien, weichere Beleuchtung und dynamische Schatten.</p>
    </article>
</div>

<script>
    window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
        if (!window.BABYLON) return;

        const retroCanvas = document.getElementById('retroCanvas');
        const modernCanvas = document.getElementById('modernCanvas');
        if (!retroCanvas || !modernCanvas) return;

        const retroEngine = new BABYLON.Engine(retroCanvas, true);
        const modernEngine = new BABYLON.Engine(modernCanvas, true);

        function addSharedComposition(scene, materialMode) {
            const ground = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateGround('ground', { width: 8, height: 8 }, scene);
            const pedestal = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateCylinder('pedestal', { diameter: 2.0, height: 0.7, tessellation: 24 }, scene);
            const hero = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateSphere('hero', { diameter: 1.45, segments: 48 }, scene);
            const leftColumn = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateBox('leftColumn', { width: 0.7, depth: 0.7, height: 2.1 }, scene);
            const rightColumn = BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateBox('rightColumn', { width: 0.7, depth: 0.7, height: 2.1 }, scene);

            ground.position.y = 0;
            pedestal.position.y = 0.35;
            hero.position.y = 1.45;
            leftColumn.position.set(-2.1, 1.05, -0.7);
            rightColumn.position.set(2.1, 1.05, 0.7);

            if (materialMode === 'retro') {
                const groundMat = new BABYLON.StandardMaterial('retroGroundMat', scene);
                groundMat.diffuseColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.73, 0.72, 0.66);
                groundMat.specularColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0, 0, 0);
                ground.material = groundMat;

                const solidMat = new BABYLON.StandardMaterial('retroSolidMat', scene);
                solidMat.diffuseColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.2, 0.45, 0.72);
                solidMat.specularColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0, 0, 0);
                pedestal.material = solidMat;
                hero.material = solidMat;
                leftColumn.material = solidMat;
                rightColumn.material = solidMat;
            } else {
                const groundMat = new BABYLON.PBRMaterial('modernGroundMat', scene);
                groundMat.albedoColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.73, 0.79, 0.82);
                groundMat.roughness = 0.9;
                ground.material = groundMat;

                const solidMat = new BABYLON.PBRMaterial('modernSolidMat', scene);
                solidMat.albedoColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.1, 0.44, 0.75);
                solidMat.metallic = 0.35;
                solidMat.roughness = 0.22;
                pedestal.material = solidMat;
                hero.material = solidMat;
                leftColumn.material = solidMat;
                rightColumn.material = solidMat;
            }

            return { ground, hero, pedestal, leftColumn, rightColumn };
        }

        function createRetroScene(engine, canvas) {
            const scene = new BABYLON.Scene(engine);
            scene.clearColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.94, 0.93, 0.89);

            const camera = new BABYLON.ArcRotateCamera('retroCam', -Math.PI / 3.4, Math.PI / 2.7, 7.1, new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1.0, 0), scene);
            camera.attachControl(canvas, true);
            camera.lowerRadiusLimit = 5.2;
            camera.upperRadiusLimit = 10;

            const light = new BABYLON.HemisphericLight('retroLight', new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1, 0), scene);
            light.intensity = 0.85;

            const objects = addSharedComposition(scene, 'retro');
            objects.ground.receiveShadows = false;

            scene.registerBeforeRender(function () {
                objects.hero.rotation.y += 0.008;
            });

            return scene;
        }

        function createModernScene(engine, canvas) {
            const scene = new BABYLON.Scene(engine);
            scene.clearColor = new BABYLON.Color3(0.89, 0.95, 1.0);

            const camera = new BABYLON.ArcRotateCamera('modernCam', -Math.PI / 3.4, Math.PI / 2.7, 7.1, new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1.0, 0), scene);
            camera.attachControl(canvas, true);
            camera.lowerRadiusLimit = 5.2;
            camera.upperRadiusLimit = 10;

            const hemi = new BABYLON.HemisphericLight('modernSky', new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1, 0), scene);
            hemi.intensity = 0.4;
            const sun = new BABYLON.DirectionalLight('modernSun', new BABYLON.Vector3(-0.6, -1, -0.4), scene);
            sun.intensity = 1.0;
            sun.position = new BABYLON.Vector3(3, 5, 2);

            const objects = addSharedComposition(scene, 'modern');
            objects.ground.receiveShadows = true;

            const shadowGenerator = new BABYLON.ShadowGenerator(1024, sun);
            shadowGenerator.useBlurExponentialShadowMap = true;
            shadowGenerator.blurKernel = 16;
            shadowGenerator.addShadowCaster(objects.hero);
            shadowGenerator.addShadowCaster(objects.pedestal);
            shadowGenerator.addShadowCaster(objects.leftColumn);
            shadowGenerator.addShadowCaster(objects.rightColumn);

            scene.registerBeforeRender(function () {
                objects.hero.rotation.y += 0.01;
            });

            return scene;
        }

        const retroScene = createRetroScene(retroEngine, retroCanvas);
        const modernScene = createModernScene(modernEngine, modernCanvas);

        retroEngine.runRenderLoop(function () { retroScene.render(); });
        modernEngine.runRenderLoop(function () { modernScene.render(); });

        window.addEventListener('resize', function () {
            retroEngine.resize();
            modernEngine.resize();
        });
    });
</script>
```
